name: mysql-schema-introspection-cache
description: A module-level UnconstrainedCache shared by every MySQLQueryTool instance (DB Query Agent's and SQL Performance Agent's, across all flows in a batch pass) caches schema-introspection results — EXPLAIN, SHOW COLUMNS/TABLES/INDEX/CREATE TABLE, DESCRIBE — because plan/schema shape is driven by schema state, not live row content. Plain SELECTs deliberately bypass it since row data can legitimately change mid-investigation.
invariants: Cache key includes resolved host:port/database, not just query text — two flows EXPLAINing the same query against different DBs must not collide
clear_schema_cache() rebinds a fresh instance (sync-callable) and must run at the top of every run_batch_once pass
